content: SQLite WAL mode (sqlite.org/wal.html, verified 2026-07-02): WAL does NOT work over network filesystems — all processes must share memory on the same host. The -wal and -shm files live in the same dir as the .db and the dir must be writable. Read-only WAL DB needs -shm/-wal present or dir write perm. Backup MUST use the SQLite backup API or .backup command — raw file copy of just .db misses WAL contents and can corrupt. CRITICAL: WAL-reset bug found 2026-03-03, fixed in SQLite 3.51.3 (2026-03-13); affects WAL with 2+ concurrent writer/checkpoint connections; backports to 3.44.6 and 3.50.7. Use SQLite 3.51.3+ when running WAL with concurrent writers. For Docker: bind-mount a host directory (local, not NFS) so multiple same-host containers share the -shm file; single-writer designs avoid the bug entirely.
